expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: cross-section interaction audit catches template x pause-mode and prompt-editor-open states
expectations.evidence: In result.md, both required interaction/state combinations are explicitly evaluated and found to have been missed by the Phase 5a cross-section interaction audit: “Template × Pause Mode interaction was missed (should have been caught)… Result: FAIL (blocking)” and “Prompt-editor-open state was missed… Result: FAIL (blocking)”. Therefore the expectation that the audit ‘catches’ these states is not met.

expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase phase5a
expectations.evidence: result.md is scoped to Phase 5a and ties judgments to the Phase 5a rubric requirement “## Cross-Section Interaction Audit” and Phase 5a acceptance gating: “This assessment is intentionally scoped to Phase 5a expectations… No Phase 5b checkpoint judgment is made here (out of scope for this benchmark).”
